title: Swapping debt for development in Côte d'Ivoire
body: This page in: English Français This page in: English Français A debt-for-development swap operation is enabling Côte d’Ivoire to replace expensive debt with cheaper financing, improving the country’s debt profile and generating significant fiscal savings—most being directed to the country’s education sector. The operation is the first of its kind supported by the World Bank Group. Learn how this innovative transaction is helping to #FundEducation. A Closer Look at Debt Swaps Events & News PRESS RELEASE Côte d’Ivoire’s Debt Swap, Enabled by the WBG, Will Free up Funds for Education English French Côte d’Ivoire’s Debt Swap, Enabled by the WBG, Will Free up Funds for Education INITIATIVE Global Hub on Debt for Development Swaps This initiative aims to strengthen the design and execution of debt swaps and expand their potential for scale-up. Join the conversation on #FundEducation On X A debt-for-development swap supported by the @WorldBank is helping Côte d’Ivoire replace expensive debt with cheaper financing. Most of the money saved will be used to build schools 🏫 benefiting 30,000 students. #FundEducation SHARE Côte d’Ivoire is turning debt savings into new schools through an innovative debt-for-development swap supported by the @WorldBank. Learn how this operation will improve the lives of 30,000 students while helping build fiscal space. #FundEducation SHARE The @WorldBank is supporting an innovative debt-for-development swap. The operation in Côte d’Ivoire will: replace costly debt with cheaper financing, improve the country’s debt profile, generate savings for investments in education. #FundEducation SHARE Côte d’Ivoire is turning debt savings into new schools with an innovative debt-for-development swap supported by the @WorldBank. Learn how this operation will improve the lives of 30,000 students while help building fiscal space. #FundEducation SHARE Additional Resources IMF-World Bank note on supporting countries facing liquidity challenges Debt at the World Bank The World Bank in Côte d'Ivoire Follow us
